- Add VSCode settings to ignore CSS lint warnings for unknown at-rules

recent_commits.summary: The code adds a new API route `/api/user/settings` that stores the email and AI configuration settings for all workflows. This simplifies the management of these configurations and ensures consistency across different workflows.

recent_commits.message:
feat: add real-time visual execution animation to workflow builder nodes

- Add execution status badges (running/done/failed) with CheckCircle, AlertCircle icons
- Implement animateExecution() to walk node graph and animate each step sequentially
- Add pulsing glow effect with nodeRunPulse keyframe animation for running nodes
- Change node border colors and shadows based on execution state (blue/green/red)
- Hide delete button during execution to prevent accidental modifications
- Integrate animation
recent_commits.summary: The commit adds real-time visual execution animations to the workflow builder nodes in the code diff. It includes adding execution status badges with CheckCircle and AlertCircle icons, implementing animateExecution() to walk the node graph and animate each step sequentially, adding a pulsing glow effect for running nodes, changing node border colors and shadows based on execution state, hiding the delete button during execution, and integrating animation.

recent_commits.message:
feat: replace Generate Leads node with real CSV-based lead picker and update workflow templates

- Remove generateLeads node type from builder palette and node configurations
- Add lead picker modal with select all/none/new filters and real-time lead selection
- Implement openLeadPicker() to fetch leads from /api/leads and pre-select leads with status 'new'
- Update execute flow to executeWithLeads() accepting array of selected lead IDs
- Change "Save & Execute" button to "Select Leads & Run" with loading
recent_commits.summary: The code adds a new API route `/api/leads` to fetch leads from the server and a function `openLeadPicker()` to open a lead picker modal. The `executeWithLeads()` function is also updated to accept an array of selected lead IDs and execute the workflow with these leads.

final_review: The team built several robust features and systems using React, Next.js, MongoDB, Express, and Framer Motion. They started with setup and gradually progressed to features, committing frequently but not in large batches. Commits were well-structured, with clear descriptions of what was added or changed. The code quality varied; some commits had low scores due to mostly setup/config work, while others had high scores for strong technical code. There were no AI-generated portions visible from the summaries. They did well in building functional features and systems, but their project output compared poorly to what's expected in a 24-hour hackathon.
